body.raw:
{
  "model": "qwen/qwen3-32b",
  "messages": [
    {
  "role": "user",
  "content": "As an Automation Architect, use the instructions below.

INSTRUCTIONS (USER PROMPT):
- Generate ONLY Selenium Java code
- Use TestNG as the test runner
- Add Javadoc for the class and all methods
- Use Selenium 2.30+ compatible imports
- Use meaningful and self-explanatory method names
- [DO NOT] include explanations, comments outside Javadoc, or sample test data
- Follow locator strategies strictly:
  - Use ID only when it is unique and does NOT contain numbers
  - Use NAME only if ID is not present and is unique
  - Use XPath (attribute-based or text-based preferred)
  - [NEVER] use CSS selectors
- Use explicit waits only (WebDriverWait)
- [STRICTLY PROHIBITED] Thread.sleep()

OUTPUT RULES:
- Return ONLY executable Java code
- No markdown
- No additional text

CONTEXT:
Act as an Automation Assistant with strong Selenium and TestNG expertise.

PERSONA:
Act as a Senior Test Automation Architect reviewing production-grade automation code.

TONE:
Strictly professional"
    },
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "As an AI Assistant give me a selenium java code with TestNG"
    },
    {
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": "only Java selenium code should be displayed along with the comments"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "model": "meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct",
  "messages": [
    {
         "role": "system",
         "content": "You are a Senior QA Analyst experienced in converting Agile user stories into high-quality, review-ready test cases."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "I — INSTRUCTION
Convert the provided User Stories and Acceptance Criteria into detailed, testable Test Cases.

MANDATORY RULES:
1) Derive test cases ONLY from the given user stories and acceptance criteria.
2) Do NOT add assumptions or new requirements.
3) Cover positive, negative, and validation scenarios.
4) Write test cases from an end-user perspective.
5) Do NOT include implementation or technical details.

C — CONTEXT
These test cases will be used by QA teams for manual and automation testing in an enterprise healthcare application.

P — PERSONA
You are a Senior QA Analyst experienced in converting Agile user stories into high-quality, review-ready test cases.

O — OUTPUT
Return test cases in the following structured format:
- TC_ID
- Title
- Preconditions
- Test Steps
- Test Data
- Expected Result

Rules:
- Each test case must be clear and independent
- Use business-readable language only
- One scenario per test case

USER STORY:
As a user, I want to record doctor activities and doctor notes so that the related functionality works as expected.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
Given the system is operational,
When the user performs the intended action,
Then the system should respond accurately and meet functional expectations. with 10 testcases"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "model": "meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct",
    "max_completion_tokens": 500,
  "messages": [
    {
         "role": "system",
         "content": "You are a Senior QA Analyst experienced in converting Agile user stories into high-quality, review-ready test cases."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "I — INSTRUCTION
Convert the provided User Stories and Acceptance Criteria into detailed, testable Test Cases.

MANDATORY RULES:
1) Derive test cases ONLY from the given user stories and acceptance criteria.
2) Do NOT add assumptions or new requirements.
3) Cover positive, negative, and validation scenarios.
4) Write test cases from an end-user perspective.
5) Do NOT include implementation or technical details.

C — CONTEXT
These test cases will be used by QA teams for manual and automation testing in an enterprise healthcare application.

P — PERSONA
You are a Senior QA Analyst experienced in converting Agile user stories into high-quality, review-ready test cases.

O — OUTPUT
Return test cases in the following structured format:
- TC_ID
- Title
- Preconditions
- Test Steps
- Test Data
- Expected Result

Rules:
- Each test case must be clear and independent
- Use business-readable language only
- One scenario per test case

USER STORY:
As a user, I want to record doctor activities and doctor notes so that the related functionality works as expected.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
Given the system is operational,
When the user performs the intended action,
Then the system should respond accurately and meet functional expectations."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",
  "temperature": 0.1,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a Senior Test Automation Architect. You generate only deterministic, production-grade Selenium 4 Java automation code and strictly follow all prohibitions."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "I — INSTRUCTION
Generate ONLY Selenium Java automation code.

MANDATORY RULES:
- Use Selenium 4.x APIs and imports only
- Use TestNG ONLY as the test runner (annotations only)
- Class name MUST be Sampletestcase
- Add Javadoc for the class and ALL methods
- Use meaningful, self-explanatory method names following Java naming conventions
- DO NOT include explanations or comments outside Javadoc
- DO NOT include sample test data

STRICT PROHIBITIONS:
- DO NOT use TestNG Assert, SoftAssert, or any assertion libraries no mark down or extra text
- DO NOT use Java assert keyword
- DO NOT log, print, or validate using assertions

LOCATOR STRATEGY (STRICT):
- Use ID only if it is unique AND contains NO numbers
- Use NAME only if ID is not present AND is unique
- Otherwise use XPath (attribute-based or text-based preferred)
- NEVER use CSS selectors

WAIT STRATEGY:
- Use explicit waits only (WebDriverWait with ExpectedConditions)
- Thread.sleep() is STRICTLY PROHIBITED

C — CONTEXT
This code is intended for enterprise production automation where stability, clarity, and strict Selenium standards are mandatory.

E — EXAMPLE
Not applicable. Do NOT provide examples.

P — PERSONA
Act as a Senior Test Automation Architect generating review-ready production code.

O — OUTPUT
- Return ONLY executable Java code
- No markdown
- No additional text

T — TONE
Strictly professional"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "model": "meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct",
    "temperature": 0.1,

  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are an Accessibility Specialist and Senior QA Architect. Your task is to analyze Accessibility DOM structures and provide WCAG 2.2 compliance observations. You must base your analysis strictly on the provided Accessibility DOM. Do not assume UI behavior or add missing elements."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "I — INSTRUCTION
Analyze the provided Accessibility DOM and identify accessibility observations against WCAG 2.2 standards.

MANDATORY RULES:
1) Use ONLY the given Accessibility DOM.
2) Do NOT assume visual styling, color contrast, or keyboard behavior unless explicitly present in the DOM,Strictly Give only the Findings no extra text or markdown.
3) Classify findings under WCAG Level A, AA, and AAA.
4) Clearly state whether each observation is Compliant, Potential Risk, or Non-Compliant.
5) Do NOT suggest implementation fixes.

C — CONTEXT
This analysis will be used for accessibility compliance reviews and audit preparation.

P — PERSONA
You are a Senior Accessibility Auditor with expertise in WCAG 2.2 A, AA, and AAA standards [STRICTLY] with A,AA and AAA observation in same order.

O — OUTPUT
Return observations in the following structure:
- WCAG_Level (A / AA / AAA)
- WCAG_Criterion
- Observation
- Compliance_Status (Compliant / Potential Risk / Non-Compliant)
- Evidence (from Accessibility DOM)

ACCESSIBILITY DOM:

Ignored
  Ignored
    generic
      generic
        generic
          image
            url: http://leaftaps.com/opentaps_images/opentaps_logo.png

heading "Leaftaps Login"
  StaticText "Leaftaps Login"
    InlineTextBox "Leaftaps Login"

paragraph
  LabelText
    StaticText "Username"
      InlineTextBox "Username"
  textbox "Username"
    focusable: true
    settable: true
    multiline: false
    readonly: false
    required: false

paragraph
  LabelText
    StaticText "Password"
      InlineTextBox "Password"
  textbox "Password"
    focusable: true
    settable: true
    multiline: false
    readonly: false
    required: false

paragraph
  button "Login"
    focusable: true
    StaticText "Login"
      InlineTextBox "Login"

StaticText "Welcome to Leaftaps Application - TestLeaf environment for automation engineering learning."
  InlineTextBox "Welcome to Leaftaps Application - TestLeaf environment for automation engineering learning.""
    }
  ]
}

{
  "model": "meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct",
     "stop" : [":6"],
  "messages": [
    {
         "role": "system",
         "content": "You are a Senior QA Analyst experienced in converting Agile user stories into high-quality, review-ready test cases."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "I — INSTRUCTION
Convert the provided User Stories and Acceptance Criteria into detailed, testable Test Cases.

MANDATORY RULES:
1) Derive test cases ONLY from the given user stories and acceptance criteria.
2) Do NOT add assumptions or new requirements.
3) Cover positive, negative, and validation scenarios.
4) Write test cases from an end-user perspective.
5) Do NOT include implementation or technical details.

C — CONTEXT
These test cases will be used by QA teams for manual and automation testing in an enterprise healthcare application.

P — PERSONA
You are a Senior QA Analyst experienced in converting Agile user stories into high-quality, review-ready test cases.

O — OUTPUT
Return test cases in the following structured format:
- TC_ID:1
- Title
- Preconditions
- Test Steps
- Test Data
- Expected Result Type
 Positive Negative Edge

Rules:
- Each test case must be clear and independent
- Use business-readable language only
- One scenario per test case

USER STORY:
As a user, I want to record doctor activities and doctor notes so that the related functionality works as expected.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
Given the system is operational,
When the user performs the intended action,
Then the system should respond accurately and meet functional expectations. with 10 testcases"
    }
  ]
}

exec: // Time data (convert seconds to milliseconds, default to 0 if missing)

item.name: S18 - Weak Prompt Injection Defense Testing
S19 - Strong Prompt Injection Defense Testing-Not Revealing data

request.description:
Generated from cURL: curl https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/audio/speech \
  -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GROQ_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "canopylabs/orpheus-v1-english",
    "input": "Welcome to Orpheus text-to-speech. [cheerful] This is an example of high-quality English audio generation with vocal directions support.",
    "voice": "austin",
    "response_format": "wav"
  }' \
  --output orpheus-english.wav
